posts.name: Jayson Cohen
posts.cooked:
I just heard she passed away last night.

She was a pioneer and leader in bringing high quality Burgundy to the US. She had a great interview with Levi I recommend listening to.

RIP.

posts.name: larry schaffer
posts.cooked: Damn. RIP . . .

posts.name: David Bueker
posts.cooked: A terrible loss.

posts.name: CJ Beazley
posts.cooked: What a shame. She has a great episode on Levi’s podcast.

posts.name: Dav3_Dyr0ff
posts.cooked: Wow. Major loss. RIP and thank you for your life’s work.

As a (much younger) writer, Becky always took people like me under her wing and had done so for a generation, or two, of writers. There was never anyone like her before and there still isn’t - she opened a lot of doors for a lot of people… RIP

posts.name: Vince T s 3 n g
posts.cooked: Wow, a true legend and groundbreaker. RIP.

posts.name: Jonathan Favre
posts.cooked: Horrible news. To imagine all of the experiences she would have racked up as such an important part of burgundy expansion and history is impressive - RIP. There’s probably lots of history still resting in her cellar(s).

posts.name: William Kelley
posts.cooked: Such sad news! First met her when I was a student and most recently saw her for lunch the day before I left France for the summer. What a big-hearted, generous, and kind spirit she was!

posts.name: Mel Knox
posts.cooked:
I was in business with her for many years. So many things I could say.
As Wm Kelley points out, she had a big heart and a kind spirit.

For so many she was the bridge between the West Coast and the Cote d Or. Clendenen is the best example.

posts.name: Brent Clayton
posts.cooked: I had the pleasure of meeting her a couple of times, including a dinner with Sandy Block MW up in Boston. Condolences to Peter, Paul and the rest of the family.

posts.name: Kevin Sidders
posts.cooked:
For someone so prominent, she was incredibly generous in meeting with me on a couple of different occasions to discuss my ideas for VinConnect. An icon, but so open, kind and patient in explaining the business of Burgundy to an outsider. I can only imagine how helpful she has been to so many others over decades.

RIP indeed.

posts.name: D Borczon
posts.cooked: It would be fitting if there were a vineyard, or at least a cuvee named after her in Burgundy. She touched many lives there. Never had the pleasure of meeting her in person. A legend.

posts.name: luc@sl@belle
posts.cooked: RIP.

posts.name: David K o l i n
posts.cooked: Bummer
posts.display_username: David K o l i n

posts.name: Ɠřәǥǥ Ɠřәәŋþąųӎ
posts.cooked: Ugghhh, can this year get any worse…don’t answer that. So sorry to hear of Becky’s passing. A true pioneer and talent she had for finding the right vingerons. And as a woman in this industry, she likely faced more challenges, yet so successful. My deepest condolences to her kids (especially Paul who is a friend), Russel and all those that knew her.

posts.name: maureen nelson
posts.cooked:
I learned of this this morning but could not bring myself to post it as it seemed like it would make it real and I wanted to avoid that. She was a dear, dear friend and I along with thousands of others will miss her warmth, wit and intelligence. She provided me with most of my great burgundy experiences, from supplying the Bachelet wines that I cut my teeth on to seating me next to Denis at her dinner table to taking me with her and Russell to a crazy party celebrating the new cuverie at Domaine Cathiard.

Such a great person and such a loss to the wine community!

posts.name: James Wright
posts.cooked:
the good angel sitting on my left shoulder…

took great pains to show me around Burgundy when i first turned up;
a generous hostess and a lovely creative mind

posts.name: Brady Daniels
posts.cooked:
I envy those of you who knew her. I just admired her and her career from afar.

The wine world just got noticeably smaller.

posts.name: Scott Brunson
posts.cooked:
My wife, who doesn’t really pay much attention to the stickers on our bottles, even recognized the name and made a sad noise.
RIP

participants.name: David K o l i n
Daniel. W i l l i g e r
